title: The reactor's support for multiple remotes per collection is what enables dual GQL + Swarm sync.
content:
## Claim
The core architectural insight enabling the Swarm channel is that the reactor's sync system supports multiple remotes per collection — each with its own channel, cursors, and push/pull state.

## Evidence
From the source: "The reactor's sync system supports multiple remotes per collection. Each remote has its own channel, its own cursors, and its own push/pull state. This means a single drive can be synced to BOTH Switchboard AND Swarm simultaneously."

In `sync_cursors`, a single collection tracks independent ordinals per remote (e.g., gql remote at ordinal 42, swarm remote at ordinal 38).

## Why it matters
Without this property, we would need a custom fan-out layer or a single combined channel. Instead, GQL and Swarm are peers, and either can be added or removed without affecting the other.

## Source
Swarm Channel Architecture, "The Core Insight: Two Channels, One Collection".
description: A single drive can sync to both Switchboard and Swarm because each remote owns its own channel, cursors, and push/pull state.
